evals.prompt: I have this screenshot of Stripe's dashboard UI. Extract the design style and generate a Tailwind theme I can use in my project. Focus on the color palette, typography, and spacing. I want both light and dark mode support.
evals.expected_output: A complete Tailwind config extending existing theme with Stripe-like colors (blue primary, clean grays), Inter/system font stack, consistent spacing scale, plus a skin.css file with CSS custom properties and dark mode support. Should include a preview HTML page.
expectations: Generates a skin.json with structured design tokens
Generates tailwind.skin.config.js with colors, fonts, spacing, shadows
Generates skin.css with CSS custom properties for light and dark mode
Generates a preview HTML file
Color palette includes primary blue, neutral grays, and semantic colors (success, error, warning)
Typography includes a system/clean font stack
All color values in the Tailwind config reference CSS custom properties for dark mode
Preview HTML demonstrates actual components styled with the skin

evals.prompt: Extract the design style from this Figma file and generate a React theme package I can use. Here's the URL: https://figma.com/design/abc123/MyApp?node-id=0-1. I want typed theme objects with a ThemeProvider.
evals.expected_output: A React theme package with typed SkinTheme object, SkinProvider component with dark/light mode support, useSkin hook, and CSS custom properties file. All values extracted from the Figma file's variables and styles.
expectations: Fetches design context from Figma using get_design_context or get_variable_defs
Generates a skin.json intermediate format
Generates skin-theme.ts with full TypeScript types
Generates SkinProvider.tsx with color mode support
Generates useSkin.ts hook
Generated code is syntactically valid TypeScript/TSX
Theme values trace back to Figma variables or extracted values

evals.prompt: I want to create a design skin that looks like Linear (the project management tool). Dark mode first, minimal, crisp. Generate CSS custom properties I can drop into any project. I don't have screenshots, just go from the general aesthetic.
evals.expected_output: A CSS-based design skin capturing Linear's aesthetic — dark backgrounds, subtle borders, clean typography, minimal shadows, accent colors. Should include variables.css and utility classes.
expectations: Generates skin.json capturing Linear's aesthetic (dark, minimal, crisp)
Generates skin-variables.css with complete token set
Dark mode is the primary/default mode
Color palette uses dark backgrounds (gray-900/950 range)
Typography uses a clean, modern sans-serif font
Includes subtle border treatments and minimal shadow usage
Generates a preview HTML that demonstrates the skin
